finding_results.notes: Replaced the only thirty-month phrasing with 'two years,' which now reconciles with the later breakdown: six months of self-management before Marcus joins, then eighteen months of Marcus handling outside distribution.
finding_results.summary: The chapter's founding/operations timeline now resolves to one consistent two-year history.

The remaining chapter 16/chapter 18 mismatch cannot be fully resolved here because this pass is restricted to chapter_16 only.
finding_results.summary: Hydrocodone remains the canonical chapter 16 medication reference, but the cross-chapter sync still needs chapter 18.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_16.md:155 now gives Lola a winding, story-shaped entry (observation about Marcus at the front desk last Tuesday, switchback to 'the whole time you were already doing this other thing'); chapters/chapter_16.md:161 replaces flat 'You didn't give us the chance' with self-correcting 'You didn't even — mijo, you didn't even ask'; chapters/chapter_16.md:185 replaces generic 'Mijo, please' with question-led indirection ('do they know you? Not your number. Do they know —') trailing into 'Mijo' when words fail.
finding_results.notes: Both exchange windows now use Lola's style-bible traits: question-led approach, indirect arrival at the point through observation/adjacent concern, self-correction switchbacks, braided speech, endearment anchoring, and high contraction level. Marcus's existing responses ('I'm sorry I didn't tell you' and 'I can't do this for love') now answer something only Lola would say rather than generic objections. Line 219 ('Ay, Dios mio') left unchanged — it functions as compressed exit affect after the fuller exchanges, not as her primary voice in the scene.

Added Carmen-specific interior beats inside both cited confrontation blocks: her licensing exposure and charting reflex, source-tracking procedural thinking, conflicted response to Marcus's money argument, and bodily attention on Lola's tremor.
finding_results.summary: Threaded Carmen's bodily and professional interiority through the TV-room confrontation so the rupture stays in close third instead of playing as detached blocking and dialogue.

pass_summaries.summary: Corrected the chapter's timeline so the origin story aligns with the six-month pre-business phase and Marcus's eighteen-month run, and preserved hydrocodone as the canonical medication reference while noting the remaining chapter 18 reconciliation as out of scope for this pass.

Revised Lola's two main speaking exchanges to restore her style-bible voice: indirect, question-led, braided, self-correcting, and endearment-rich. First exchange (line 155) now opens with a winding observation about Marcus visiting last week before pivoting to the betrayal; her follow-up (line 161) uses a characteristic mid-sentence restart. Second exchange (line 185) replaces a flat plea with questions about whether his new contacts know him as a person, trailing off into the endearment when the thought exceeds what she can finish. Scene outcome, leverage shifts, and all other characters' lines unchanged.
Added Carmen-centered interior beats through the Marcus confrontation, varied the chapter's pressure language, and re-landed the close on Carmen's unfinished overnight shift.
